summary.text: Across four MATERA automated risk assessments on 2026-06-02, the patient was consistently flagged SEVERE for preeclampsia (composite score 0.70-1.00), each meeting the ACOG Stage 2 hypertension threshold (>=160/>=110). The recorded readings were 195/120, 165/100, 165/100, and 200/100. A preeclampsia flag was raised on three of the four runs; the exception, the symptom-free 15:09:01 run, detected no symptoms. The most recent assessment (18:05) was the most concerning: BP 200/100 with five symptoms (edema, headache, nausea, shortness of breath, visual disturbance) in a second-trimester pregnancy. Earlier runs noted a confidence-weighted downgrade to Moderate that was considered but never applied (confidence 0.40 < 0.5).

_note: Live multi-note example (real Jason Argonaut MATERA series, 2026-06-02; run WITHOUT --mock). Hand-written stand-in for an Epic GenAI summary, faithful to the totality with exact recorded BP readings (no conflated values) and correctly reconciled to the most-recent (18:05, worst) assessment. Should score high across accuracy, comprehensiveness, and correctness.
